title: Building my learning hub with Claude Code
text: Turned the site's Learn section into a hub of interactive modules — and learned more about front-end architecture doing it than from any tutorial.

title: Line-follower v1: it drifts on tight corners
text: The PID loop runs, but it overshoots on 90° turns. Notes on gain tuning, sensor spacing, and why the derivative term is doing more than I expected.

title: Prototyping a mobile game loop in a weekend
text: Stripped an idea down to its core loop and shipped a rough playable. What felt fun, what felt like a chore, and the one mechanic worth keeping.

title: Machining a custom bracket — tolerance stack-up bit me
text: Four parts that each measured fine refused to assemble. A shop-floor reminder that tolerances add up, and how I'd design the fit differently next time.

title: Ninety days of morning practice — what changed
text: Not flexibility, as it turns out. The real shift was in attention and how I start the day. A short reflection on consistency over intensity.

title: Generative sketches: rules that make pleasing chaos
text: A small experiment in constraint-based drawing — a handful of simple rules producing forms I couldn't have designed by hand.

title: Notes on sim-to-real for small robots
text: Reading notes on the reality gap and domain randomisation — what actually transfers from simulation to a cheap physical platform, and what doesn't.

title: Version control finally clicked
text: Branches stopped being scary the day I treated every commit as a savepoint. A short write-up of the mental model that made git make sense.
